name: HITMAN™
description: There is a world beyond ours. Beyond nations, justice, ethics. It never sleeps. It exists everywhere. And once you enter....there is no going back. Welcome to the World of Assassination. You are Agent 47, the world's ultimate assassin.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • OS *: OS 64-bit Windows 7
  • Processor: Intel CPU Core i5-2500K 3.3GHz / AMD CPU Phenom II X4 940
  • Memory: 8 GB RAM
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 660 / Radeon HD 7870
  • DirectX: Version 11
  • Storage: 50 GB available space
comment1:
This is basically a puzzle game wearing a very expensive suit and pretending to be a stealth game. And I mean that as a compliment.

The best part is how open every mission feels. You get dropped into these big, detailed locations, and the game just lets you figure out how professional or completely ridiculous you want to be. You can follow a clean plan, steal the right disguise, learn the target’s routine, and walk out like nothing happened. Or you can spend twenty minutes holding a wrench, dressed as someone who definitely should not be there, hoping nobody asks difficult questions.

I love how much replay value the levels have. The first run is usually just survival and confusion. Then you come back, notice new paths, new disguises, new accidents, new opportunities, and suddenly the same map feels like a different mission. That’s where the game really shines.

It’s smart, funny in a very dry way, stylish, and weirdly relaxing once you stop panicking. Agent 47 may be the only man alive who can look calm while carrying a fish, a screwdriver, and several bad ideas.
comment2: It really sucks that this game cant be bought on the steam store anymore. Its still a really great game!
comment3: Best stealth game I've ever played, on par with Dishonored
